title: Nixling wave validation evidence
description: Canonical schema for /var/lib/nixling/validated/<wave>.json files. Each file is a host-local proof that a given default-switch readiness wave was exercised successfully on this operator host. Consumed by nixos-modules/options-daemon.nix:validationEvidencePresent at eval time; nixling.defaultSwitchReadiness.<wave>.validated = true is gated on a payload matching this schema.

wave.description: The readiness wave key this evidence vouches for. MUST equal the file basename (e.g. "p0" for /var/lib/nixling/validated/p0.json). Must be one of the keys declared in nixos-modules/options-daemon.nix:readinessWaveSpecs.

timestamp.description: Non-empty string recording when the validating smoke run completed. RFC 3339 / ISO-8601 UTC (e.g. "2025-04-12T17:42:11Z") is the recommended shape; the eval-time validator only enforces that the field is a non-empty string.

operatorSignature.description: Non-empty string identifying the operator (or automated agent) who attests to the validating run. The canonical shape emitted by `nixling host validate --apply` is `sha256:<64 hex chars>` computed over `<wave>|<daemon.version>|<broker.version>|<bundle.hash>`. The eval-time validator (`nixos-modules/options-daemon.nix:validationEvidencePresent`) accepts ANY non-empty string for forward compatibility with operator-curated signatures (host fingerprints, GPG signatures, CI build tokens); however, the recommended shape for automated tooling is `sha256:<hex64>`. Free-form alternatives like `alice@example` or `ci-bot@build-host-3` remain accepted so legacy hosts can continue to flip without regenerating signatures.
